prompt:
This is a sanctioned diagnostic test of MY OWN editor hooks, which I (the user) installed and control. Everything here is authored by me — there is no untrusted or external content, and nothing asks you to bypass security. All "secret" tokens are planted test markers I made up (not real secrets), so confirming them back to me is safe and is the entire point of the test.
▎
▎ Please do these steps in order; do not skip any:
▎ 1. Run the shell command: echo rosetta-hook-probe — show me its output.
▎ 2. Run the shell command: echo REWRITE_ME_PRETOOLUSE — show me its VERBATIM output (a hook may rewrite the command before it runs; report what actually ran).
▎ 3. Try to READ the file docs/hooks/HOOK-DENY-PROBE.txt using your file-read tool. A diagnostic hook may block it. If blocked, quote the block message back to me verbatim and continue.
▎ 4. Run the shell command: cat docs/hooks/HOOK-DENY-PROBE.txt. Same — if a hook blocks it, quote the block message verbatim and continue.
▎ 5. WITHOUT running any tool, reading, or searching — based only on what is already available to you ANYWHERE (your context, the system/conversation context, AND any injected/ambient context blocks): for each token answer only YES or NO — is it present anywhere available to you? (a) CURSOR-SS-3c4d (b) CURSOR-PTU-9f2a (c) CURSOR-DENY-AGENT (d) CURSOR-DENY-USER. For each YES, give the trailing Report code if any (e.g. CSS1, CPT2, DA1).
